title: Module Decomposition SSOT
purpose: Generate package layout, import rules, ports, and daemon boundary docs from an executable module manifest plus go list evidence.
decisions: The only deployable binary in this repository is cmd/riido.
SaaS server code stays in riido-control-plane; deploy/apply/evidence code stays in riido-infra; shared DTO/schema facts stay in riido-contracts.
Claude, Codex, OpenClaw, and Cursor CLIs are external attached resources and are never bundled, installed, or silently downloaded.
Domain packages depend inward on contracts and ports. Provider, local IPC, filesystem, process, SaaS HTTP, and host/store behavior enter through adapters.

Store App GUI code is not a daemon domain package. A future desktop/app repository may own UI and OS entitlement adapters, while riido-daemon owns C11 contracts, helper runtime, local IPC server, and store distribution gates.
Future Store App GUI adapter ownership must remain an outer adapter concern, never a daemon domain dependency.

factor_boundary.configuration: RIIDO_* env vars or explicit local CLI flags
factor_boundary.state: Local daemon state is disposable; durable facts live in task DB JSON, sidecar lease/registry files, mwsd projection files, workdir metadata, or SaaS assignment events.
factor_boundary.listener: cmd/riido opens local IPC only and must not add a public HTTP listener.

change_procedure: Update this manifest if the package/dependency map changes.
Update docs/20-domain/context-map.md if bounded-context ownership changes.
Add or update a focused public workflow when the boundary can be checked in GitHub Actions without secrets or provider binaries.
assertions: The reader package map is generated from this manifest and checked against go list package existence.
Production imports for listed package groups must not match forbidden internal prefixes.
cmd/riido is the composition root; domain packages stay behind ports and adapters.
Future GUI/store app ownership remains an outer adapter concern.
